notes: Realistic public profiles for the 5 FP&A competitors monitored by Scenario A. URLs are real production endpoints — Firecrawl will hit these in the demo. Source diversity is intentional: tests the system on dense data (Pigment, Anaplan) and sparse data (Drivetrain).

competitors.name: Pigment
competitors.tagline: Business planning for the AI era.
competitors.positioning: Modern, collaborative FP&A platform with strong scenario planning and an aggressive AI roadmap. Pitches itself as 'Anaplan for the AI era'.
competitors.ideal_customer: Series B+ tech companies, mid-market enterprises with finance teams of 5-50.
known_weaknesses: Implementation still 6-10 weeks
Pricing opaque — gated quote process
Limited integrations outside core ERP set
Steep learning curve for non-finance users
competitors.demo_notes: Use Pigment as the 'HIGH signal' competitor in the demo — they ship frequently and have lots of public data. Their pricing page is the cleanest delta-detection example.

competitors.name: Anaplan
competitors.hq: Miami, FL, USA (post-2022 Thoma Bravo take-private)
competitors.positioning: Enterprise-grade connected planning. Heavy in finance, supply chain, and workforce. Target buyer is the Fortune 1000 CFO.
competitors.ideal_customer: Enterprises with >2000 employees and complex multi-entity planning.
known_strengths: Workforce + supply chain modules beyond finance
known_weaknesses: Reputationally slow innovation post-Thoma Bravo
Implementation 4-9 months — partner-led, expensive
User experience dated vs Pigment / us
License costs >€150k/yr typical entry point
competitors.demo_notes: Anaplan is the legacy giant we displace. The 'Mertonbridge Logistics' deal in the mock CRM is currently on Anaplan — perfect for showing how the system surfaces displacement context.

competitors.name: Planful
competitors.tagline: The financial performance platform for mid-market.
competitors.positioning: Cloud FP&A for the mid-market. Originally Host Analytics. Strength in close + consolidation.
competitors.ideal_customer: Mid-market US companies, 200-2000 employees, often in services or finance verticals.
known_strengths: Close + consolidation depth (legacy of Host Analytics)
known_weaknesses: Slower product velocity vs Pigment / us
Weaker scenario modelling
EU presence thin — limited data residency options
UX feels 2018
competitors.demo_notes: Planful blog occasionally publishes vertical expansion posts (e.g., PE portfolio) — good MEDIUM-tier delta example.

competitors.name: Drivetrain
competitors.tagline: AI-native financial planning for high-growth companies.
competitors.positioning: AI-first FP&A built for SaaS metrics. Targets Series A-C software companies who outgrew spreadsheets.
competitors.ideal_customer: Series A-C SaaS, US-centric, 50-300 employees with one or two finance hires.
known_strengths: Pre-built SaaS metric library (ARR, NRR, magic number)
Direct integrations with Stripe, HubSpot, Salesforce
known_weaknesses: No public pricing — every quote bespoke
No public changelog — opacity on velocity
EU presence near-zero
Smaller team — perceived risk for enterprise buyers
competitors.demo_notes: Drivetrain is the SPARSE-DATA edge case. No pricing page, no changelog. The system should yield LOW priority and a confidence score under 50 — demonstrate this honestly in the demo.

competitors.name: Vena
competitors.tagline: Excel-powered FP&A on a modern cloud platform.
competitors.positioning: Excel-native FP&A. Pitches itself as 'don't change your finance team's habits, give them a better engine underneath'. Microsoft-aligned.
competitors.ideal_customer: Mid-market US/UK/CA finance teams who live in Excel and don't want to migrate.
known_strengths: Excel-native — zero retraining for finance team
known_weaknesses: Excel-native is a ceiling — modelling complexity capped by Excel itself
Limited collaboration features vs Pigment / us
EU presence small
Clunky scenario comparison UX
competitors.demo_notes: Vena's positioning is the 'different angle' — when a deal mentions strong Excel attachment (like Quizora Forms in mock CRM), Vena is the threat. Good test for nuanced competitive context.
